title: Template: Optimizing Computer Vision for Edge Devices
excerpt: A complete template demonstrating every available block type in our new Next.js JSON blog architecture, including GitHub cards, tables, and blockquotes.

content.value: When deploying machine learning models to edge devices, the constraints shift dramatically from raw accuracy to inference speed and memory management. This template demonstrates how to structure a technical write-up using all available JSON block types.
Hardware constraints breed software elegance. An optimized edge model doesn't just run faster; it forces the engineer to truly understand the data flowing through the network.
Before diving into the code, it is crucial to establish a baseline. We evaluate models based on their quantized size and latency.

| Model Architecture | Float32 Size | INT8 Size | Inference Time (ms) |
|---|---|---|---|
| ResNet-50 | 98 MB | 25 MB | 145 ms |
| MobileNetV2 | 14 MB | 3.5 MB | 32 ms |
| Custom CNN (Ours) | 5 MB | 1.2 MB | 12 ms |
A flowchart diagram showing the image processing pipeline from camera capture to MQTT transmission.

The real optimization happens during frame pre-processing. If we can crop and threshold the image before it hits the neural network, we save precious compute cycles. You can view the full preprocessing script in the repository below:

import cv2
import numpy as np
import tflite_runtime.interpreter as tflite

def process_frame(frame):
    # Convert to grayscale to reduce channel depth
    gray = cv2.cvtColor(frame, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)
    
    # Resize to match model input
    resized = cv2.resize(gray, (224, 224))
    
    # Normalize pixel values
    normalized = resized.astype(np.float32) / 255.0
    
    # Add batch dimension for inference
    input_data = np.expand_dims(normalized, axis=0)
    return input_data
Before flashing this to your IoT device, ensure you have completed the following steps to guarantee stable uptime:

value: Verify thermal throttling limits on the target hardware.
Test the INT8 quantized model against the validation set to check for severe accuracy degradation.
Ensure the camera buffer is being flushed correctly to prevent memory leaks.
Set up a daemon service (like systemd) to auto-restart the Python script on crash.
